metadata.title: Local Integration Test: 6-Factor Factorial — Post-Training + Analysis + Biostatistics
metadata.status: planned — depends on post-training and analysis XML plans
metadata.priority: P0 — validates 3-flow pipeline locally before cloud GPU run

context.summary:
Local integration test exercising the COMPLETE 6-factor factorial design (4×3×2×2×2×4 = 384 conditions) on CPU-only hardware. The expensive GPU Model Training Flow (Flow 2, Layer A) ran on GCP; its artifacts are downloaded to local disk via make sync-cloud-artifacts.
This test exercises ALL 6 factor dimensions: Layer A factors (#1-#3): model_family, loss_name, aux_calibration (from cloud) Layer B factors (#4-#5): post_training_method, recalibration (run locally) Layer C factor (#6): ensemble_strategy (run locally) Layer D analytical factors (#7-#13): specification curve (run locally)
Layers B+C+D run on local CPU — cost is ZERO. Only Layer A cost money (~$65 on GCP).
This validates the "decoupling" feature: GPU training on cloud, CPU post-processing on researcher's laptop/desktop. The full factorial is NOT just the 24 training conditions — it is 384 conditions (debug) or 720 conditions (production) across all 6 experimental factors.
Prerequisites: 1. GCP debug factorial run completed (24 conditions × 1 fold, Layer A) 2. Artifacts synced to local disk via make sync-cloud-artifacts (Issue #882) 3. Local mlruns/ populated with all training run artifacts
The test uses the LOCAL factorial-monitor skill for orchestration.
context.synthesis-reference:
This plan is subordinate to the Pre-Debug Run Synthesis: docs/planning/intermedia-plan-synthesis-pre-debug-run.md
Key synthesis sections governing this plan: - Part 1.2: All 4 layers of the factorial design - Part 1.3: MVRD scope — 384 debug conditions across all 6 factors - Part 2.1: Flow execution order (Post-Training → Analysis → Biostatistics) - Part 2.2: Inter-flow discovery via MLflow tags (not filesystem paths) - Part 2.3: MLflow experiment names (4 experiments) - Part 2.4: MLflow tag schema - Part 4.3: What needs to happen next (local validation) - Part 7.1: Complete 6+7 factor table (6 experimental + 7 analytical)
context.full-factorial-context:
The complete 6-factor design (from pre-gcp-master-plan.xml): 4 models × 3 losses × 2 aux_calib × 3 post-training × 2 recalibration × 5 ensemble = 720
The factorial YAML is COMPOSABLE via Hydra config groups (configs/factorial/*.yaml). Users create their own configs — paper_full.yaml and debug.yaml are just examples.
The YAML structure is sectioned by layer: factors: training: # Layer A (GPU, cloud) — factors #1-#3 model_family: [dynunet, mambavesselnet, sam3_topolora, sam3_hybrid] loss_name: [cbdice_cldice, dice_ce, dice_ce_cldice] aux_calibration: [true, false] post_training: # Layer B (CPU, local) — factors #4-#5 method: [none, swa, multi_swa] recalibration: [none, temperature_scaling] analysis: # Layer C (CPU/GPU) — factor #6 ensemble_strategy: [none, per_loss_single_best, all_loss_single_best, per_loss_all_best, all_loss_all_best]
ALL factors are auto-derived from whichever factorial YAML was used. Zero hardcoded factor names in infrastructure code.
context.factorial-context:
This integration test exercises ALL 6 experimental factor dimensions:
Layer A (Training, already complete on cloud — cost: ~$65): #1 model_family (4): dynunet, mambavesselnet, sam3_topolora, sam3_hybrid #2 loss_name (3): cbdice_cldice, dice_ce, dice_ce_cldice #3 aux_calibration (2): true, false Subtotal: 4×3×2 = 24 training cells × 1 fold = 24 runs
Layer B (Post-Training, exercised locally — cost: ZERO): #4 post_training_method (2 debug / 3 full): none, swa [, multi_swa] #5 recalibration (2): none, temperature_scaling Subtotal: 24 × 2 × 2 = 96 post-training variants
Layer C (Analysis, exercised locally — cost: ZERO for DynUNet): #6 ensemble_strategy (4 debug / 5 full): per_loss_single_best, all_loss_single_best, per_loss_all_best, all_loss_all_best [, none] Subtotal: 96 × 4 = 384 evaluation conditions
Layer D (Biostatistics, exercised locally — cost: ZERO): #7-#13: analytical choices (metric, aggregation, mcc_method, alpha, rope, test_type, effect_size) All parameters from BiostatisticsConfig (Rule #29), never hardcoded. Layered ANOVA: core 3-way → extended 5-way → full 6-factor ensemble analysis.
TOTAL DEBUG: 4×3×2×2×2×4 = 384 conditions × 1 fold = 384 evaluations TOTAL PRODUCTION: 4×3×2×3×2×5 = 720 conditions × 3 folds = 2160 evaluations

mlflow-experiments.experiment: Contains: 24 training runs + up to 96 post-training variants. Post-training logs to SAME experiment as training (synthesis Part 8 Q1).
Contains: up to 384 analysis evaluations (debug) + zero-shot baselines. Analysis creates runs in SEPARATE experiment (synthesis Part 2.3).
Contains: statistical analysis artifacts (DuckDB, Parquet, figures, LaTeX).

decoupling-architecture.cloud-gpu: Model Training Flow (Flow 2) — SkyPilot + Docker + GCP L4 spot Produces: checkpoints, per-fold metrics, resolved_config.yaml THIS IS THE ONLY LAYER THAT COSTS MONEY.
decoupling-architecture.local-cpu: Post-Training Flow (Flow 2.5) — SWA, calibration, conformal (CPU-only) Expands 24 training runs → 96 post-training variants Analysis Flow (Flow 3) — ensemble building, evaluation, UQ (CPU-only for DynUNet) Expands 96 variants → 384 evaluation conditions Biostatistics Flow (Flow 5b) — statistical analysis (pure CPU) Layered ANOVA on all 6 factors
decoupling-architecture.artifact-sync: make sync-cloud-artifacts — downloads mlruns/ from cloud MLflow Requires: MLFLOW_CLOUD_TRACKING_URI or GCS bucket access Manual at this point (P2 issue #882 for advanced sync)

context.execution-order: 1. Download Layer A artifacts from cloud → local mlruns/ (make sync-cloud-artifacts) Input: 24 training runs from GCP 2. Post-Training Flow (Layer B) on ALL 24 training conditions (CPU) → Creates 96 post-training variants in minivess_training → Applies factors #4 (post_training_method) and #5 (recalibration) 3. Analysis Flow (Layer C): build ensembles, evaluate, compute UQ (CPU) → Creates 384 evaluation runs in minivess_evaluation → Applies factor #6 (ensemble_strategy) 4. Biostatistics Flow (Layer D): layered ANOVA, spec curve, rank stability (CPU) → Creates statistical artifacts in minivess_biostatistics → Analyzes all 6 factors with layered ANOVA structure 5. Verify: all statistical outputs correct, all figures generated, all 384 conditions covered
context.inter-flow-discovery: Post-Training discovers Training: find_upstream_safely(experiment_name="minivess_training", upstream_flow="training-flow") Analysis discovers Training + Post-Training: EnsembleBuilder.discover_training_runs() queries minivess_training Biostatistics discovers Analysis: discover_source_runs(experiment_names=["minivess_evaluation"])
context.layered-anova-structure:
The ANOVA progression:
1. Core 3-way ANOVA (Layer A factors only): model_family × loss_name × aux_calibration Answers: "Which training factor matters most for segmentation quality?"
2. Extended 5-way ANOVA (Layers A+B): + post_training_method × recalibration Answers: "Does post-training (SWA, temperature scaling) improve over raw checkpoints?"
3. Full 6-factor ensemble analysis (all layers A+B+C): + ensemble_strategy Answers: "What is the optimal ensemble strategy? Does ensembling help?"
All factors auto-derived from the factorial YAML that was used. Zero hardcoded factor names in ANOVA code. All statistical parameters from BiostatisticsConfig (Rule #29).

phase.name: Artifact Download + Verification
phase.description: Download Layer A results from GCP cloud and verify all 24 training conditions are present. These 24 runs are the INPUT to the local Layers B+C+D pipeline.

tasks.task: Implement make sync-cloud-artifacts Makefile target. Downloads mlruns/ from cloud (GCS or RunPod) to local disk. Uses gsutil rsync or mlflow artifacts download.

Verify: all 24 training conditions present in local mlruns/. Check: each run has checkpoints, metrics, tags. Tags must match synthesis Part 2.4 schema (model_family, loss_function, fold_id, with_aux_calib, flow_name). These 24 runs are the Layer A grid: 4 models × 3 losses × 2 calibrations.
Verify: DVC data available locally for evaluation. MiniVess validation volumes + DeepVess test volumes.

phase.gate: All 24 Layer A training conditions discoverable from local mlruns/.

phase.name: Post-Training Flow — Layer B (Local CPU, Cost: ZERO)
phase.description:
Applies Layer B factors: #4 post_training_method: {none, swa} (2 debug levels) #5 recalibration: {none, temperature_scaling} (2 levels) to all 24 training conditions. Creates new runs in minivess_training (SAME experiment — synthesis Part 2.3 + Part 8 Q1).
Expansion: 24 training runs × 2 × 2 = 96 post-training variants. Factor levels read from configs/factorial/debug.yaml::factors.post_training.

tasks.task: Run post_training_flow on each of 24 training conditions. Apply SWA plugin (average across checkpoints within each condition). Apply temperature_scaling on conditions with_aux_calib=True. Use MINIVESS_ALLOW_HOST=1 for local Docker bypass (pytest only). Debug scope: {none, swa} × {none, temperature_scaling} = 4 variants per condition. Total: 24 × 4 = 96 post-training variants.

Verify: new MLflow runs created in minivess_training with correct tags. Tags: post_training_method={method}, upstream_training_run_id={id}. All upstream tags inherited (model_family, loss_function, fold_id, with_aux_calib). Factor values from factorial YAML, not hardcoded.
Verify: SWA checkpoints are valid (loadable, same architecture as original). Verify: calibration parameters stored as artifacts.

phase.gate: Post-training creates 96 new MLflow runs (24 × {none, swa} × {none, temp_scaling}).

phase.name: Analysis Flow — Layer C (Local CPU, Cost: ZERO for DynUNet)
phase.description:
Applies Layer C factor: #6 ensemble_strategy: 4 debug levels (per_loss_single_best, all_loss_single_best, per_loss_all_best, all_loss_all_best) to all 96 training + post-training variants. Creates evaluation runs in minivess_evaluation (SEPARATE experiment — synthesis Part 2.3).
Expansion: 96 variants × 4 ensemble strategies = 384 evaluation conditions. This completes the full debug factorial: 4×3×2×2×2×4 = 384. Factor levels read from configs/factorial/debug.yaml::factors.analysis.

tasks.task: Run analysis_flow discovering all 24 training + 96 post-training runs from minivess_training. Build all 4 ensemble strategies. Include post-training variants as ensemble members. Use MINIVESS_ALLOW_HOST=1 for local Docker bypass. Total evaluations: 384 conditions + zero-shot baselines.
Verify: per-volume metrics logged for MiniVess validation. Check eval/{fold}/vol/{vol_id}/{metric} entries in MLflow. Per-volume metrics are ESSENTIAL for Biostatistics (Wilcoxon, spec curve).
Verify: DeepVess external test evaluation completes. Check test/deepvess/all/{metric} entries. ALL ensemble strategies evaluated on DeepVess (Rule #27 — no debug scope reduction).
Verify: UQ from deep ensembles computed (for all_loss_all_best). Debug: summary statistics only (mean epistemic per volume). Production: full 5D maps stored as artifacts (synthesis Part 8 Q2).
Verify: champion selection using compound_masd_cldice metric. Champion metric from config (BiostatisticsConfig), not hardcoded.
Verify: zero-shot baselines (sam3_vanilla, vesselfm) evaluated as standalone conditions, tagged with is_zero_shot="true".
Verify: ensemble logit-level averaging (NOT metric-level). _EnsembleInferenceWrapper: torch.stack(outputs).mean(dim=0).

phase.gate: Analysis creates 384 correctly tagged evaluation runs in minivess_evaluation.

phase.name: Biostatistics Flow — Layer D (Local CPU, Cost: ZERO)
phase.description:
Applies Layer D analytical choices (#7-#13) to all 384 analysis runs. All statistical parameters from BiostatisticsConfig (Rule #29). Creates artifacts in minivess_biostatistics.
The ANOVA is LAYERED to match the factorial design layers: 1. Core 3-way ANOVA: factors #1-#3 (model_family × loss_name × aux_calibration) 2. Extended 5-way ANOVA: + factors #4-#5 (post_training_method × recalibration) 3. Full 6-factor ensemble analysis: + factor #6 (ensemble_strategy)
All 6 factor names are auto-derived from the factorial YAML — zero hardcoded names.

tasks.task: Run biostatistics_flow discovering all 384 analysis runs from minivess_evaluation. Compute LAYERED ANOVA: 1. Core 3-way: model_family × loss_name × aux_calibration (Layer A factors) 2. Extended 5-way: + post_training_method × recalibration (Layers A+B) 3. Full 6-factor: + ensemble_strategy (all layers A+B+C) K=1 debug → per-volume replication (synthesis Part 1.3 MVRD). Fold treated as random effect when K>1 (production). All factor names auto-derived from factorial YAML, not hardcoded.

Verify: specification curve analysis completes. Iterates over ALL researcher degrees of freedom (Layer D factors #7-#13). Check: permutation p-value computed. All parameters from BiostatisticsConfig (alpha, rope_values, etc.).
Verify: rank stability analysis completes. Check: Kendall's tau matrix for all metric pairs. The clDice vs DSC rank inversion IS a paper finding (synthesis Part 1.4).
Verify: calibration metrics computed (Brier, O/E, IPA). Reads calibration artifacts from post-training runs.

Verify: DuckDB populated with all 384 conditions. Schema includes all 6 factorial columns from layers A+B+C. The 6 columns: model_family, loss_name, aux_calibration, post_training_method, recalibration, ensemble_strategy. Export Parquet files for reproducibility.
Verify: figures and tables generated (LaTeX, Markdown). TRIPOD+AI mapping complete.

Verify: pairwise comparisons with two-tier MCC. Co-primary (clDice, MASD): Holm-Bonferroni correction. Secondary (DSC, HD95, etc.): BH-FDR correction. MCC method configurable via BiostatisticsConfig.co_primary_metrics.
Verify: Bayesian signed-rank with ROPE (baycomp). ROPE values from BiostatisticsConfig.rope_values (per-metric), not hardcoded.

phase.gate: Complete layered statistical analysis produced from all 384 local conditions.

phase.name: End-to-End Verification — All 6 Factors
phase.description: Verify the full 6-factor factorial pipeline ran correctly from cloud artifacts through local Layers B+C+D. Total: 384 debug conditions.

tasks.task: Verify: factorial-monitor skill tracks all 3 local flows. Check: all flows reported as SUCCEEDED. Check: total condition count = 384 (4×3×2×2×2×4).
Run make test-staging to verify no regressions from local run.

Verify: inter-flow tag discovery works end-to-end across all 6 factors. Post-Training reads training tags from minivess_training (factors #1-#3). Analysis reads training + post-training tags (factors #1-#5). Biostatistics reads analysis tags (all 6 factors #1-#6). All tag names match synthesis Part 2.4 schema. All factor names auto-derived from factorial YAML.

Verify: the layered ANOVA correctly partitions variance across all 6 factors. Core 3-way answers: "Which training factor matters most?" Extended 5-way answers: "Does post-training improve over raw checkpoints?" Full 6-factor answers: "What is the optimal ensemble strategy?"
Document: local run time, disk space used, any issues found. This feeds into the cold-start prompt for the full 4-flow GCP debug run.

Verify: a different lab could run the same pipeline with a custom configs/factorial/my_lab.yaml containing different factor levels. The infrastructure code never hardcodes factor names or level counts.

phase.gate: All 384 conditions verified. All 3 local flows complete. Ready for full GCP production run (720 conditions × 3 folds = 2160 evaluations).

known-blockers.blocker: train_flow.py bypasses Hydra-zen. Uses argparse + 9-key dict instead of compose_experiment_config(). MLflow runs lack config/resolved_config.yaml. Workaround: factorial SkyPilot YAML passes all args explicitly.
Training logs loss_name but ensemble builder reads loss_function. Fallback chain exists in builder.py:229-234 but is fragile.
post_training_out volume missing from docker-compose.flows.yml. Impact: post-training artifacts lost on container exit.
No standardized mechanism to download MLflow artifacts from cloud to local disk. make sync-cloud-artifacts target needs implementation (Phase 0).

deterministic-script.description: The .sh script that orchestrates this local integration test. Uses LOCAL factorial-monitor skill for tracking. Must invoke Prefect flows (Rule #17 — never standalone scripts).

appendix.title: Complete Factor Table (synthesis Part 7.1)
appendix.content:
This integration test exercises ALL factors across all 4 layers:
EXPERIMENTAL FACTORS (6 — define the 720-condition / 384-debug grid):
| # | Factor | Layer | Full Levels | Debug Levels | Compute | Config Section | |---|-------------------------|--------------------|-------------|-------------|-------------|------------------------| | 1 | model_family | A (Training) | 4 | 4 | GPU (GCP) | factors.training | | 2 | loss_name | A (Training) | 3 | 3 | GPU (GCP) | factors.training | | 3 | aux_calibration | A (Training) | 2 | 2 | GPU (GCP) | factors.training | | 4 | post_training_method | B (Post-Training) | 3 | 2 | CPU (local) | factors.post_training | | 5 | recalibration | B (Post-Training) | 2 | 2 | CPU (local) | factors.post_training | | 6 | ensemble_strategy | C (Analysis) | 5 | 4 | CPU (local) | factors.analysis |
Full product: 4 × 3 × 2 × 3 × 2 × 5 = 720 conditions × 3 folds = 2160 evaluations Debug product: 4 × 3 × 2 × 2 × 2 × 4 = 384 conditions × 1 fold = 384 evaluations
Cost: ONLY Layer A runs on GPU (~$65 for 72 runs). Layers B+C+D run on local CPU (free).
ANALYTICAL FACTORS (7 — researcher degrees of freedom, Layer D):
| # | Factor | Levels | Config Source | |---|-------------|-------------|----------------------------------------| | 7 | metric | 8+ | BiostatisticsConfig.metrics | | 8 | aggregation | 2 | spec curve engine | | 9 | mcc_method | 2 | BiostatisticsConfig.co_primary_metrics | | 10| alpha | configurable | BiostatisticsConfig.alpha | | 11| rope | per-metric | BiostatisticsConfig.rope_values | | 12| test_type | 4+ | spec curve engine | | 13| effect_size | 3 | biostatistics_statistics.py |

appendix.title: Factorial Expansion Math — From 24 Training Runs to 384 Debug Evaluations
appendix.content:
Layer A (Training, GPU cloud, ~$65): 4 models × 3 losses × 2 aux_calib = 24 cells × 1 fold (debug) = 24 training runs
Layer B (Post-Training, local CPU, ZERO cost): 24 runs × {none, swa} × {none, temperature_scaling} = 24 × 2 × 2 = 96 variants
Layer C (Analysis, local CPU, ZERO cost for DynUNet): 96 variants × 4 ensemble strategies = 384 evaluation conditions
Layer D (Biostatistics, local CPU, ZERO cost): Layered ANOVA on all 384 conditions across all 6 factors
Debug total: 4 × 3 × 2 × 2 × 2 × 4 = 384 conditions + zero-shot baselines (sam3_vanilla, vesselfm) evaluated separately
For PRODUCTION: Layer A: 24 × 3 folds = 72 runs (~$65) Layer B: 72 × 3 × 2 = 432 variants (free) Layer C: 432 × 5 = 2160 evaluations (free for DynUNet) Full factorial: 4 × 3 × 2 × 3 × 2 × 5 = 720 × 3 folds = 2160

appendix.title: Composable Factorial YAML — Why Factor Names Are Never Hardcoded
appendix.content:
The factorial YAML is COMPOSABLE via Hydra config groups. Users create their own configs/factorial/*.yaml files. The platform provides named examples:
configs/factorial/paper_full.yaml — 720 conditions (Nature Protocols) configs/factorial/debug.yaml — 384 conditions (pipeline validation)
A different lab creates configs/factorial/my_lab.yaml: factors: training: model_family: [dynunet, sam3_hybrid] # 2 models only loss_name: [dice_ce] # 1 loss only aux_calibration: [false] # no calibration post_training: method: [none] # no post-training recalibration: [none] # no recalibration analysis: ensemble_strategy: [per_loss_single_best, all_loss_all_best] # 2 strategies
Result: 2×1×1×1×1×2 = 4 conditions — runs in minutes on a laptop. All infrastructure code (flows, ANOVA, spec curve) works unchanged.

synthesis.relation: authoritative synthesis — this plan is subordinate
